Notes: Standardized for residential/private networks. Good for local DNS.
Intended for testing. Safe choice for local/dev environments.
Best for sample configs and docs, not the best choice for real deployments.
Valid, but meant for multicast DNS. Avoid using it as a normal unicast DNS suffix if mDNS is present.
Refers to the local host, not other machines on the LAN.
Useful when you want a name that should never resolve.

Typical use: Internal label in Kubernetes DNS names
Recommendation: Do not use as a standalone suffix
Notes: Common in names like service.namespace.svc.cluster.local; svc is a label in Kubernetes DNS, not a standard private TLD.

Official status: Not currently listed as a finalized IANA special-use domain
Recommendation: Not recommended yet as a standard
Notes: IANA announced a provisional determination in 2024, but it is not shown in the current special-use list checked.
Common in practice, but not standardized or reserved.
home.arpa was standardized to replace older non-standard .home usage.

Recommendation: Do not use for local/private DNS
Notes: Real public TLD, so it can conflict with public DNS behavior and browser expectations.

Recommendation: Do not use as private-only suffixes
Notes: Better to use a subdomain you control, such as internal.example.com.
